title: Uniface as a DDE Server
text: Uniface as a DDE Server When Uniface acts as a DDE server, the DDE link must be defined in the client application. When you next select the Paste Link command in a program that can act as a DDE client, a DDE link is established. After that, whenever the linked data changes in Uniface, it changes in the client application. For example, if the client is a worksheet application with a link to Uniface, the worksheet app attempts to refresh the linked data. The client application can only obtain data from Uniface when it is running and the referenced component is current. If Uniface is not running, the client application may offer to start it. Unfortunately, it cannot run the correct component automatically. Only the " field.entity " item syntax is supported for poke transactions. Poke transactions cannot operate on fields that have focus. Execute transactions result in the receiveMessage trigger being fired. The command string is available in $result . Related Links DDE Syntax Rules Programmed Links If you want to define links with multiple fields or even multiple occurrences, you must use a programmed link. This is because it is not possible to select more than one field in Uniface for copying to the Microsoft Windows clipboard. DDE Links Applications that communiicate via DDE do so using links that can be defined by copying and pasting them to the destination application or programming them using the DDE syntax rules.
